And while, as you can see, I am quite behind in posting (I apologize- the rest are coming soon, I promise)- I rolled into Owen Sound, Ontario late last night-the ultimate terminus on the Underground Railroad. I’m hunkering down for a couple of days to soak it all in, before heading down to Ann Arbor, Michigan. My final destination. Whew. What a ride. My legs need a little rest. Keep in touch on here. Hearing from each of you makes my day. Love to you all! Marking another piece of history. Tracing route of underground railroad. @humansoftheUR shared her journey from U-S to #OwenSound. (if my voice isn’t too terribly annoying when I listen to it, I’ll post the Podcast when it is available as well) “We came here back in high school and after we were here, our principal was really impacted by this painting. He wanted it up in our school. So he got transparencies of it. The both of us were interested in art, so we helped out and painted it at our high school. It took us two to three months, and it was a lot of work. But it is still there today. We came back today toIt's incredible how Ashtabula has left its mark on you! I'm thrilled that your journey across Ohio all the way up into Canada via Owen Sound continues capturing moments etched by history—a testament indeed when one traces routes along with those who sought freedom through secret networks like The Underground Railroad did so long ago. Your dedication in recreating art inspired during high school days speaks volumes about its significance; spending months ensuring every brushstroke resonates within the halls is admirable! texts.full_text: Sri Lankan Sinhala Love Story website that includes allSri Lankan Sinhala Wal Kata stories, images, and videos in one place for access and browsing. The site aims to provide a comprehensive collection of various Sinhala Wal Kata content from different sources like blogs, social media platforms, and websites, all curated under one roof. Users can explore multiple categories, ranging from classic tales to modern-day anecdotes, and enjoy the rich culture and heritage of Sri Lanka through its diverse wal kata stories. To make navigation easier, the website is divided into several sections: 1. Home: A quick overview of recent posts, trending topics, and popular categories to give users a glimpse of what's new in the world of Sinhala Wal Kata. 2. Website Categories: An organized list of various genres like romance, comedy, horror, and more, allowing users to explore their preferred type of content with ease. 3. Contact: A dedicated page for users to reach out with feedback, suggestions, or even share their wal kata stories for potential publication on the site. 4. Search: An intuitive search bar that helps users find specific topics, authors, or keywords related to Sinhala Wal Kata within the website's vast database. [The research progress on the chemical constituents and pharmacological activities of genus Gentiana]. Genus Gentiana belongs to the family Gentianaceae, which contains about 400 species distributed in temperate regions of the world. The Chinese Pharmacopoeia (2015 edition) has recorded five kinds of medicinal materials from this genus: Longdancao (Radix Gentianae), Maobiancao (Radix Swertiae), Qinbulao (Radix Gentianae Macrophyllae), Zicao (Radix Gentianae Oligocephalae) and Tianqi (Rhizoma Gentianae). The chemical constituents of the genus Gentiana are mainly iridoids, flavonoids, xanthones, secoiridoids, triterpenoids, alkaloids, phenolic acids, etc. In recent years, many studies have been carried out on the chemical constituents and pharmacological activities of this genus. This paper summarizes the research progress on the chemical constituents and pharmacological activities of Gentiana species in recent years, which provides a reference for further development and utilization of this genus. texts.full_text: rnard Delfont, Baron Delfont (5 September 1909 – 28 July 1994), born Boris (or Boruch) Winogradsky, was a leading Russian-born British theatrical impresario. Life and career Delfont was born in Tokmak, Russian Empire, the second son of Isaac and Olga Winogradsky. His brothers Lew Grade and Leslie Grade also entered showbusiness. His nephew Michael Grade (now Lord Grade of Yarmouth), Leslie's son, has had a career in the television and film industries. He had a sister, Rita Grade, who wrote a book about the family called "My Fabulous Brothers." His family was Jewish. Delfont entered theatrical management in 1941, after a career as first a dancer, then an agenct for his brother Lew's dance troupe The Dancers of Harlem. He became managing director and chairman (from 1969) of the Stoll Moss group from its formation until it merged with EMI to form Thames Television. He was knighted in January 1976 "for services to British theatre" by Queen Elizabeth II, who had been a patron at his theatres on many occasions. He became Baron Delfont (of Stepney) of Kensington upon London and the City of Westminster later that year; he took as motto for his coat-of-arms: 'The show must go on'. He was married twice: 1976, to Joan Mary Tabor Delfont died in Kensington aged eighty-four. ## External links and sources Wikimedia Commons has media related to bernard Delfont. * Obituary: Lord (Bernard) Delfont -

texts.full_text: pediatric practice (estimates of incidence range from 9 to 23% of the one to three month old population), it is not well understood and there are few effective treatments. Clinical reports and results of laboratory research strongly support the use of rhythmic, rocking motion, or rocking-vibration to sooth infants who often have fussy, colicky episodes. Even with this knowledge about the effectiveness of motion/vibration, there is no commercially available product to provide soothing motion/vibration aimed at easing colicky episodes in infants. The specific aims of this research are, 1) to better understand and document characteristics of colic in infants so that recommendationcan be made about the specific motion/vibration characteristics that are most soothing, 2) to develop and clinically test the motion/vibration characteristics that are most soothing to infants, and 3) to develop and clinically test the motion/vibration characteristics that are most soothing to infants and that are most effective in reducing the duration and frequency of colicky episodes in infants. By the end of the study, the research team aims to develop and clinically test the most effective soothing motion/vibration characteristics that can be implemented in a commercially available product to help infants and their caregivers. Dahlberg, 942 F.2d 328, 340 (6th Cir.1991) ("Any uncertainty whether the courts ... will consider petitioner's habeas claim on the merits is not substantial enough to render resort to the state process futile.)) The panel in Hannah then ignores the stated rule and presumes that the defendant will not be able to pursue his claims in state court. The majority here adopts this questionable holding for the instant case and then misapplies it. The conclusion that Kentucky state courts would refuse to hear Haeberlin's claims because they were not all brought in his first 11.42 motion and are thereby procedurally defaulted ignores the fact that Kentucky state courts have previously allowed multiple Rule 11.42 motions by the same party. While the Kentucky courts usually apply the "one bite" rule, they have already demonstrated their willingness to deviate from the procedural rules by hearing Haeberlin's two Rule 11.42 motions in this case. This Court, therefore, should not decide for the Kentucky state courts how they would handle a further claim by Haeberlin. Whether the Kentucky state court would entertain another motion on the issues here is unknown. See, e.g., Ferguson v. Murphy, Washington, D. C., appointed by this court, argued the cause for appellant. With him on the brief was James A. Doyle, Jr., Washington, D. C. John R. Koerner, Asst. U. S. Atty., with whom Earl J. Silbert, U. S. Atty., John A. Terry and David G. Fiske, Asst. U. S. Attys., were on the brief, for appellee. Before ROBINSON, Circuit Judge, and FAHY and BURGER, Senior Circuit Judges. BURGER, Senior Circuit Judge: Appellant was convicted of possession of heroin in violation of 21 U.S.C. § 844(a) (1970). He contends that the trial court erred in denying his motion to suppress evidence seized during a search of his person and in admitting into evidence statements made by him after he was arrested but before he was advised of his Miranda rights. We affirm. I On March 2, 1972, at approximately 3:00 m., Officer James E. Johnson of the Metropolitan Police Department was on duty and in uniform patrolling an area near 14th Street and Park Road, N. W., when he observed standing with two other men in front of a liquor store. Appellant was talking to one of the men while the third man stood The officer testified that as he approached the group, he saw appellant hand something to the man with whom he had been conversing; the latter then handed it back to appellant and walked away. Officer Johnson further testified that he observed appellant place his right hand in his pocket and remove a object which he placed in his mouth. Officer Johnson immediately approached appellant and asked him what he had in his mouth. Appellant replied, "Nothing." The

texts.full_text: view of these demands, Japanese Patent Publication Tokkai 6-267709 disclosed thermistor chips of a layered structure obtained by stacking up one on top of another a plurality of elements each having a positive temperature characteristic and having electrodes formed on both its main surfaces, attaching them by using an electrically conductive adhesive and connecting the individual elements in parallel. Thermistor chips with a low resistance value can be obtained by structuring them in this manner. When thermistor chips with such a structure are to be produced, not only is it necessary to attach the individual elements with an adhesive such that their electrodes overlap each other but also to keep the electrodes on each element in a mutually insulated relationship. Thus,it becomes essential during production processes like stacking and bonding for these thermistor chips. One challenge involves ensuring precise alignment of overlapping electrode areas while maintaining insulation between them across different layers or elements within an assembly process that may involve high temperatures due to the use of adhesives with specific curing requirements. This can be particularly complex when dealing with miniaturized components where even slight misalignments could lead to short circuits. To address these challenges, advanced techniques and materials are employed in manufacturing processes: 1. Precision Alignment Systems: Utilizing automated alignment systems that incorporate vision-guided technology helps ensure accurate placement of each element during stacking operations. 2. Insulating Layers or Coatings: Applying thin insulative layers between electrodes can prevent electrical shorts while still allowing for adhesive bonding. 3. Advanced Adhesives with Controlled Conductivity and Insulation Properties: Developing adhesives that provide both necessary conductivity to connect elements in parallel, as well as insulation properties where needed ensures the integrity of each element's individual functionality withi

Significant differences between the control group with problem-solving skills training and positive reappraisal coping intervention group ![Describing the stages of performing the intervention](ijrb-15-687-g001){#F1} Discussion ========== In this study, the problem-focused coping strategies were found to have increased in the PSS training group. In the study by Goorhan and colleague, after two sessions of counseling for problem-focused coping strategies among the women undergoing the treatment, there was no statistically significant difference between the intervention and the control groups ([@B25]). In that study, one consultation session was held as a group session and one consultation session was held with each individual. The exclusion criteria were not being under psychiatric treatment and the lack of psychiatric disorders during the study. However, in the present study, PSS training was given individually during three sessions. Also, the general health score above 28 was the exclusion criteria, which can affect the study results. In the study by Heidari and colleague, there was a relation between general health and coping strategies in infertile women. Infertile people who used problem-focused coping strategies had attained higher general health in comparison to the infertile people who used less of the problem-focused coping strategies ([@B6]). In our study, the coping strategies of thoughtful problem-solving had increased in the PSS training group. In the study of Zanuzyan and colleague, after seven sessions of PSS training, the mean scores of coping strategies of thoughtful problem-solving in the PSS training group were significantly increased compared to the control group ([@B11]). This is aligned with the result of the current study about the effect of the PSS training on problem-focused coping strategies. texts.full_text: ars to define the beginning stages of what might have been intended for use within Rich Site Summary (RSS) version 0.91 or similar standards from Netscape Communications in their "MyNetspace" platform. It includes: - An `<?xml>` declaration specifying encoding as ISO8652. This is a standard way to define the character set used by an XML document, ensuring proper rendering of special characters across different systems and locales. The DOCTYPE statement indicates that this content relates specifically to RSS (Rich Site Summary) version 0.91. Within comments: - There's information about whatRSS stands for: RichSiteSummary. This is a metadata format used primarily in online publications, allowing users or aggregators like news readers and feed parsers to extract details such as article titles, summaries, publication dates etc., from various sources into an organized view (like MyNetscape). - It mentions that RSS enables display of "channels" on theMy Netspace website. This suggests it's part of a system where users could create their own channels or pages aggregating content like news articles and displaying them in this platform, similar to how modern feed readers work but tailored specifically for use within MyNetscape portal at its peak usage time around 1998-2003. The references provided are likely intended as documentation sources: ``` RSS Info can be found athttp://my.nets cape.com/publish/ XML Infocanbefoundat http: //www.w3.org/XML/ ``` These URLs would have been where developers could go to learn more about creating RSS feeds and XML standards in general. The copyright is noted as belonging Netscape Communications 1985 (assuming typo from user input), but given the context, it's likely a placeholder or an older version reference. The contact email provided (`danda@nets cape.com`) would have been for Dan Libby at that time; he was known to be involved in early RSS development. Finally,it notes this DTD is based on one originally created by Lars Marius Gai..., which might indicate the original creator of some aspects or earlier versions, but his name seems cut off

texts.full_text: court has determined that this opinion should not be published and is not precedent except under the limited circumstances set forth in 5TH CIR. R. 47.5.4. Case: 17-10721 Document: 00514529754 Page: 2 Date Filed: 06/26/2018 No. 17-10721 1317816 (Apr. 16, 2018) (No. 17-8114). The new-trial motion was filed while the direct appeal was pending. We review the denial of a new-trial motion for abuse of discretion. United States v. Pratt, 807 F.3d 641, 645 (5th Cir. 2015). “Questions of law are reviewed de novo, but the district court’s findings of fact must be upheld unless they are clearly erroneous.” Id. (internal quotation marks and citation omitted). Rule 33 motions are not favored. Id. The Nandas argue that the Government promised two testifying codefendants that they would not be immediately deported and that the promise was not revealed until sentencing, when the Government sought to modify a restitution award to preclude the codefendants’ convictions from qualifying as “aggravated felonies.” The Nandas also assert that the codefendants testified falsely, with the Government’s knowledge, that they had not been promised a particular immigration result. The codefendants’ plea agreements stated that the Government could not promise a particular immigration result. Moreover, prior to trial, the Government accurately disclosed the intent of the agreements. Thus, the district court did not clearly err by concluding that there was no undisclosed promise. Further, the district court did not clearly err by finding that the codefendants did not testify falsely as to their understanding of their plea agreements, even if that understanding may have been incomplete in some respects. See Pratt, 807 F.3d at 645; see also United States v. Dunnigan, 507 U.S. 87, 94 (1993); United States v. Simpson, 741 F.3d 539, 555 (5th Cir. 2014). Finally, even were we to assume there was undisclosed impeachment evidence or false testimony, there is no reasonable likelihood that it affected the verdict. See United States v. Stanford, 823 F.3d 814, 838-39 (5th Cir. 2016). The judgment is AFFIRMED.

texts.full_text: s, MHC class I antigen presentation and processing genes (the immunoproteasome LMP2 and LMP7 and the transporter TAP1 and TAP2) seem to be closely linked in a primordial organization. Two distinct lineages of class Ia and LMP7 loci were previously identified, thus strongly suggesting co-evolution among 'class I region' genes. We now show that the Xenopus MHC 'class I region' lies between class II and class III genes and we have isolated two distinct alleles at both the TAP1 and TAP2 loci. The alleles at each locus are remarkably divergent from each other and phylogenetic tree analysis revealed in both cases that they diverged from each other 60-100 million years ago (MYA). For lineage-frequency and linkage analysis, 25 wild-caught X. laevis and 13 F(1) progeny of a cross between two homozygous MHC haplotypes were genotyped for TAP1 and TAP2 alleles by PCR-RFLP. The results show that the two lineages are in strong linkage disequilibrium with each other and with class Ia loci, but not with class II genes. This is further evidence for co-evolution among 'class I region' genes. The transporter associated with antigen processing (TAP) gene encodes a heterodimeric protein complex which translocates peptides from the cytosol into the endoplasmic reticulum where they are loaded onto MHC class I molecules. We have isolated and characterized TAP1 and TAP2 cDNA clones from Xenopus laevis, an amphibian species that has been used as a model system for studying immune responses in lower vertebrates. The deduced amino acid sequences of X. laevis TAP1 and TAP2 show 60% identity to human TAP1 and TAP2, respectively. In addition, the predicted secondary structure of the transmembrane domains is highly conserved between Xenopus and mammalian TAP proteins. texts.full_text: underwent standard sPECT imaging to evaluate the impact of corrections on lesion detection and quantification. Group C comprised 14 patients with intermediate CAD likelihood, serving as a validation cohort to assess clinical utility and reproducibility of the corrected images. Methods: - Correction Algorithms: A combined approach was employed, integrating iterative reconstruction with motion compensation (based on ECG-gated respiratory and cardiac motion tracking) and resolution recovery using a point-spread function (PSF)-based model. Attenuation correction was performed using a CT-derived attenuation map. - Quantitative Analysis: Bull’s-eye plots were generated for all groups, with and without corrections. Key metrics included defect size (% of myocardium), maximum transmural count ratio, and regional wall motion scores. It took us 25 years to get accepted as Pink Floyd fans because people thought maybe I was trying too hard. “However, on this last tour everyone who came seemed genuinely blown away by what has happened in BritFloyd and how good we’ve got.” Darlington founded the Australian based act with his wife – guitarist Joanne Darlinton - at first to keep alive their love for Pink Floyd. Although they have always done shows beyond Australia’s shores, it wasn’t until 2011 when Damian realised that things were happening in Europe which could take them there. With only six weeks before leaving the band would return from a UK tour he knew how hard getting BritFloyd off of Australian ground was going to be: “It turned out I had taken on way more than anticipated and it took me some time - two years later – for us finally get things in place with all our logistics, musicians etc. “And because we were starting at the bottom again as far Europe goes but once you break through that barrier of ignorance then its really good.” What has impressed Darlington during their first trip to North America is just how open minded fans have been: “We are playing shows from San Diego and down in Los Angeles.
